page.title: Introduction to the Internet of Things

content.text:
Module:
Introduction to the Internet of Things  |
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UET1
VHS:
21h  |
Weekly hours:
1h30  |
Lectures:
1h30  |
TD:
-  |
TP:
-
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
1  |
Credits:
2
Teaching Objectives
Explain the technical foundations and challenges of the Internet of Things
Identify IoT components, protocols and architectures
Implement simple applications using communication between objects
Recommended Prerequisites
Basics in computer networks and object-oriented programming concepts
Course Content (Lectures: 21h)
Introduction to the Internet of Things (definitions, challenges, history)
Components and sensors in IoT (sensors, actuators, microcontrollers)
Network architectures and communication models (client-server, publish/subscribe)
IoT communication protocols
Use cases and application domains (health, agriculture, industry, smart city)
Security, privacy, and standardization norms
IoT platforms (e.g. Arduino, Raspberry Pi, Node-RED, AWS IoT...)
Connected object programming (Java, C, Python)
IoT, Big Data and ambient intelligence: challenges and perspectives
IoT, innovation and industrial ecosystems
Assessment
Exam: 100%
Bibliographic References
Pierre-Jean Benghozi, Sylvain Bureau, Françoise Massit-Folléa, L'Internet des Objets. 2009.
Adrian McEwen, Hakim Cassimally, Designing the Internet of Things, Wiley, 2013.
Olivier Hersent, L'Internet des objets : Les principaux protocoles M2M, Dunod, 2014.
Samuel Greengard, The Internet of Things, MIT Press, 2015.
Tomás Domínguez-Bolaño et al., An overview of IoT architectures, technologies, and existing open-source projects, Volume 20, 2022. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.iot.2022.100626
